name: French Chocolate Loaf Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Adjust rack about 1/3 up from the bottom of the oven.
2. Line a 6-cup capacity loaf pan with foil, wrinkling as little as possible. Brush the foil with melted butter or spray with Pam just before pouring batter.
3. Resift cornstarch three times and set aside.
4. Chop chocolate and place in a heavy-bottomed saucepan. Dissolve coffee in boiling water and pour over chocolate. Cover and melt on low heat, stirring with a whisk until smooth. Set aside to cool slightly.
5. In a bowl of large mixer, beat butter until soft. Gradually add sugar and beat for 2-3 minutes, scraping the bowl as needed.
6. Add egg yolks one at a time, beating well. Continue to beat until the mixture is pale and creamy.
7. Add chocolate, then cornstarch, and beat only until smooth, scraping the bowl as needed.
8. In another bowl, beat egg whites with salt until they just stand up; do not overbeat. Gradually add the whites to the chocolate mixture, folding gently but completely.
9. If using Pam, spray now and pour batter into the pan. Lift the pan in both hands and move it gently back and forth to even out the batter.
10. Place the pan (which may not be deeper than the cake pan) in a larger pan filled with about an inch of boiling water. Bake for 50-55 minutes, or until a tester inserted into the middle comes out clean and dry.
11. Turn off the heat, open the oven door a few inches, and let the cake sit inside for 20 minutes to cool slightly.
12. Open the door all the way and let the cake stand in the oven for another hour until room temperature is reached.
13. Remove the cake from the water bath and dry the cake pan. Invert onto a serving plate and remove foil. Serve upside down or freeze. Optionally, cover with chocolate curls sprinkled with powdered sugar or whipped cream and berries.
